title: मंगल दोष निवारण स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री मंगल दोष निवारण स्तोत्रम् ॥

अतुलितबलधामं हेमशैलाभदेहं
दनुजवनकृशानुं ज्ञानिनामग्रगण्यम्।
सकलगुणनिधानं वानराणामधीशं
रघुपतिप्रियभक्तं वातजातं नमामि॥ १॥

मंगलं भगवान् विष्णुः मंगलं गरुडध्वजः।
मंगलं पुण्डरीकाक्षो मंगलायतनं हरिः॥ २॥

मंगलग्रहदोषघ्नं हनुमन्तं महाबलम्।
प्रणमामि सदा भक्त्या मंगलं मे प्रयच्छतु॥ ३॥

भूमिपुत्रो महातेजाः मंगलो मंगलप्रदः।
महिषासुरमर्दिन्याः कृपायाः पात्रमस्तु मे॥ ४॥

धरणीगर्भसम्भूतं विद्युत्कान्तिसमप्रभम्।
कुमारं शक्तिहस्तं तं मंगलं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ५॥

लोहितो लोहिताक्षश्च सामगानां कृपाकरः।
धरात्मजः कुजो भौमो मंगलो भूमिनन्दनः॥ ६॥

मंगलाय मंगलदाय मंगलेशाय मंगलम्।
मंगलेशं प्रपद्येऽहं मंगलं तनुते सदा॥ ७॥

हनूमानञ्जनासूनुर्वायुपुत्रो महाबलः।
रामेष्टः फाल्गुनसखः पिंगाक्षो अमितविक्रमः॥ ८॥

नमो हनुमते तुभ्यं मंगलग्रहपीडितम्।
त्रायस्व सर्वदोषेभ्यो मंगलं कुरु सर्वदा॥ ९॥

भीमरूपं महावीरं कपिराजं सुरार्चितम्।
मंगलग्रहदोषघ्नं नमामि पवनात्मजम्॥ १०॥

॥ इति श्री मंगल दोष निवारण स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Mangal Dosh Nivaran Stotram ||

Atulita-bala-dhamam hema-shaila-abha-deham
danuja-vana-krishanum jnaninam-agra-ganyam |
Sakala-guna-nidhanam vanaranam-adhisham
Raghupati-priya-bhaktam Vata-jatam namami || 1 ||

Mangalam Bhagavan Vishnuh mangalam Garuda-dhvajah |
Mangalam Pundarikaksho mangalayatanam Harih || 2 ||

Mangala-graha-dosha-ghnam Hanumantam Mahabalam |
Pranamami sada bhaktya mangalam me prayachchhatu || 3 ||

Bhumi-putro maha-tejah Mangalo Mangala-pradah |
Mahishasura-mardinyah kripayah patram-astu me || 4 ||

Dharani-garbha-sambhutam vidyut-kanti-sama-prabham |
Kumaram Shakti-hastam tam Mangalam pranamamy-aham || 5 ||

Lohito lohitakshash-cha Sama-ganam kripa-karah |
Dharatmajah Kujo Bhaumo Mangalo Bhumi-nandanah || 6 ||

Mangalaya Mangala-daya Mangalesha-ya Mangalam |
Mangalesham prapadye'ham mangalam tanute sada || 7 ||

Hanuman-Anjana-sunur-Vayu-putro Mahabalah |
Rameshtah Phalguna-sakhah Pingaksho Amita-vikramah || 8 ||

Namo Hanumate tubhyam Mangala-graha-piditam |
Trayasva sarva-doshebhyo mangalam kuru sarvada || 9 ||

Bhima-rupam Maha-viram Kapi-rajam Surarchitam |
Mangala-graha-dosha-ghnam namami Pavana-atmajam || 10 ||

|| Iti Shri Mangal Dosh Nivaran Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
अतुलित बल के धाम, सुवर्ण पर्वत के समान देह वाले, राक्षसों के वन को जलाने वाले अग्नि, ज्ञानियों में अग्रगण्य, सकल गुणों के निधान, वानरों के अधीश, रघुपति के प्रिय भक्त, पवनपुत्र को नमन॥ १॥

भगवान विष्णु मंगलमय हैं, गरुडध्वज मंगलमय हैं। पुण्डरीकाक्ष मंगलमय हैं, हरि मंगल का आश्रय हैं॥ २॥

मंगल ग्रह के दोष का नाश करने वाले महाबली हनुमान को सदा भक्ति से प्रणाम करता हूँ, वे मुझे मंगल प्रदान करें॥ ३॥

भूमिपुत्र, महातेजस्वी, मंगलप्रद मंगल। महिषासुरमर्दिनी की कृपा का पात्र मैं बनूँ॥ ४॥

धरणी के गर्भ से उत्पन्न, विद्युत के समान कान्ति वाले, शक्ति हाथ में धारण किए कुमार मंगल को प्रणाम करता हूँ॥ ५॥

लोहित, लोहिताक्ष, सामगानों पर कृपाकर, धरात्मज, कुज, भौम, मंगल, भूमिनन्दन — ये सब नाम हैं॥ ६॥

मंगल को, मंगलदाता को, मंगलेश को मंगल हो। मंगलेश की शरण लेता हूँ, सदा मंगल का विस्तार हो॥ ७॥

हनुमान, अञ्जनासुत, वायुपुत्र, महाबली, राम के प्रिय, अर्जुन के सखा, पिंगाक्ष, अमितविक्रम॥ ८॥

हे हनुमान! आपको नमस्कार। मंगल ग्रह से पीड़ित की सब दोषों से रक्षा करो, सदा मंगल करो॥ ९॥

भीमरूप, महावीर, कपिराज, देवताओं द्वारा पूजित, मंगल ग्रह दोष का नाश करने वाले पवनात्मज को नमन॥ १०॥
benefits: Powerful remedy for Mangal Dosh (Mars affliction) in horoscope
Reduces the malefic effects of planet Mars
Traditionally recited for removal of obstacles in marriage caused by manglik dosha
Invokes courage and removes fear and anxiety
Best recited on Tuesdays for maximum benefit
description: Dedicated to Lord Hanuman, the Mangal Dosh Nivaran Stotram (मंगल दोष निवारण स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise revered in Hindu tradition. Attributed to Jyotish Shastra - Traditional, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to bless with harmonious married life and remove obstacles in marriage caused by Manglik dosha. It is also said to reduce the malefic effects of planet Mars. This sacred text is ideally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and during Hanuman Jayanti for enhanced blessings. At 10 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 7 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Mangal Dosh Nivaran Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Mangal Dosh Nivaran Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Powerful Hanuman prayer to remove Mangal Dosha and Mars affliction from horoscope.
relatedSearches: Mangal Beej mantra Om Kram Kreem Kraum

seoFaqs.question: What is Mangal Dosh and why is the stotra recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Mangal Dosh, also called Manglik Dosh or Kuja Dosha, is a Vedic astrological condition arising when Mars (Mangal) is placed in the 1st, 4th, 7th, 8th, or 12th house of the natal chart. Traditional astrology associates this placement with delays in marriage, friction in relationships, health concerns, and obstacles in career stability. The Mangal Dosh Nivaran Stotram and accompanying mantras are recited as upaya (remedy) to invoke Mangala Deva and reduce the malefic effects of Mars in the horoscope. (Source: Pandit N M Shrimali; Online Puja.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the core mantra recited in this remedy?
seoFaqs.answer: The principal beej mantra used in Mangal Dosh Nivaran is 'Om Kraam Kreem Kraum Sah Bhaumaaya Namah,' addressed to Bhauma, the son of the Earth, an epithet of Mars. The Vedic mantra 'Om Angarakaya Namah' (salutations to Angaraka, the burning ember) is also chanted. Traditional prescriptions recommend 108 daily repetitions for 40 days starting on a Tuesday, ideally during shukla paksha, with red sandal, red flowers, masoor dal, and a lit oil lamp before an image of Mangala Deva or Hanuman. (Source: IIA Group; Mahakaal Prasad.)

seoFaqs.question: How many times should the stotra be chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: Standard upaya prescribes 108 repetitions of the Mangal beej mantra daily for 40 consecutive Tuesdays, totalling roughly 4,320 repetitions across the anushthana. For a more intensive remedy known as Mangal Mahapuja, a homa is performed in which the mantra is offered into the sacred fire 10,000 times along with sesame, ghee, and barley. Astrologers may also recommend a one-time 1,25,000 (sava lakh) japa under priestly supervision for severe Mangal Dosh combinations. (Source: Ask Ganesha; Pandit N M Shrimali.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Tuesday the prescribed day for this remedy?
seoFaqs.answer: In the Vedic vara-system, each weekday is governed by a graha; Tuesday (Mangalwar) is ruled by Mars. Performing remedies on the planet's own day amplifies the resonance between the worshipper and the deity. Tuesday is also the day of Hanuman, who in tradition is considered the antidote to Mars-related troubles because of his mastery over Mars-like qualities of strength, courage, and aggression. Many devotees combine Hanuman Chalisa recitation with the Mangal stotra on Tuesdays for compounded benefit. (Source: Online Puja; Ask Ganesha.)

seoFaqs.question: What other ritual elements accompany the Mangal stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: Besides japa, traditional Mangal Shanti includes wearing red coral (moonga) set in copper or gold after consultation with an astrologer, donating red lentils (masoor dal), red cloth, copper, and jaggery on Tuesdays, fasting on Tuesdays, visiting a Mangal Nath or Hanuman temple, and performing an Angarak Shanti puja at Ujjain's Mangal Nath Mandir, considered the navel point of Mars. The stotra anchors all these acts as the verbal core of the remedy. (Source: Mahakaal Prasad; Vedic Vaani.)
